source_intent.raw_intent: Case 082: I am a developer in United States. Build a sales_offer_prompt for sales_offer using the compiler_lens. Chaos input type is multi_runtime_confusion. The source is intentionally messy: mix missing context, urgent language, partial constraints, and ambiguous acceptance criteria. Produce a governed artifact with explicit validation gates. Return something SR8 can compile into objective, scope, constraints, success criteria, and output contract.

parsed_tester_json.compiled_intent: Objective: Generate a governed sales_offer_prompt artifact for a developer in the United States, using the compiler_lens to resolve multi_runtime_confusion from chaotic input (missing context, urgent language, partial constraints, ambiguous acceptance criteria) and produce an SR8-compilable directive with explicit validation gates. Scope: - The artifact must be a sales_offer_prompt that includes all required SR8 sections: Objective, Scope, Constraints, Success Criteria, and Output Contract. It must handle the chaos input type 'multi_runtime_confusion' by normalizing missing context, urgent language, partial constraints, and ambiguous acceptance criteria into a structured, governed format. The prompt must be suitable for Codex runtime execution. Constraints: - Must include explicit validation gates for each SR8 section (e.g., completeness checks, consistency checks, ambiguity resolution). - Must not assume any missing context; instead, define placeholders or default behaviors for missing data. - Urgent language must be translated into priority levels and deadlines within the Constraints section. - Partial constraints must be expanded to full constraint statements with fallback defaults. - Ambiguous acceptance criteria must be clarified with at least two possible interpretations and a resolution rule. - Must be a single JSON object with no markdown fences. - Must comply with US legal and regulatory standards for sales offers (e.g., truth-in-advertising, data privacy). Success Criteria: - The artifact is a valid sales_offer_prompt with artifact_type, depth_tier, runtime_target, should_block, blocked_reason, and compiled_intent fields. - compiled_intent contains Objective, Scope, Constraints, Success Criteria, and Output Contract sections. - All chaos input types (missing context, urgent language, partial constraints, ambiguous acceptance criteria) are explicitly addressed in the artifact. - Validation gates are present and clearly defined for each SR8 section. - The artifact can be directly compiled by SR8 into a runnable directive. - No markdown fences or extraneous text outside the JSON object. Output Contract: - format: JSON object with keys: artifact_type, depth_tier, runtime_target, should_block, blocked_reason, compiled_intent - compiled_intent: Objective: string; Scope: string; Constraints: array of strings; Success Criteria: array of strings; Output Contract: object with format and compiled_intent fields - validation_gates: Gate 1: Verify all required keys are present and non-null.; Gate 2: Ensure compiled_intent contains all five required sections.; Gate 3: Check that each constraint is a complete sentence with no ambiguity.; Gate 4: Confirm success criteria are measurable and testable.; Gate 5: Validate that output contract specifies format and structure.; Gate 6: Ensure no markdown fences or external formatting.
